rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YU-TIEN LEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee And; Confirm Asset To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/08/2023. (1) This 850 Petition needs to be filed in a trust case by opening a new case as the court had asked before. (2) Petition claims interest in the real property that is being allegedly held in the predeceased wife's estate, not this estate. Petition may need to clarify who the Respondent that holds the predeceased spouse's 50% interest at this time. (3) Notice of Hearing filed 1/25/2023; however, insufficient. DE-115 Notice of Hearing must be used and Notice of Remote Appearance to be given as well. (4) Clarification if the personal representative of this case has considered or will consider filing spousal property petition in the other case. PC 13650. PC 13540. (5) Amended Petition to include all exhibits. CRC 7.3(3). In doing so, please clarify the attached Full Reconveyance to the Accessor-Recorder's letter dated 7/17/2023 is related to this property or not.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANCINE MARIE DOUGLASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar: 1) Nothing has been filed to address the examiner's notes last updated and e-mailed to counsel on 11/02/2022. The only document that has been filed since the last hearing on 01/30/2023 is a DE-120 form (which is an incorrect form to give notice of this petition) without a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6). 2) No proof of service of notice to the San Francisco Public Administrator having higher priority for appointment is on file, despite the Court's directive to give such notice (see minutes of 01/30/2023 hearing). 3) The petitioner does not appear to have standing in his individual capacity. If the petitioner is opening this probate to pursue the creditor claim on file in his representative capacity as successor trustee, a new petition must be filed by the petitioner in his trustee capacity and as a creditor, although the creditor claim was filed more than one year after the decedent's death and appears to be time-barred under Probate Code section 9352 and CCP section 366.2. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
